title: Better Industrial REIT: Stag Industrial or EastGroup Properties?
author: James Halley
description: The article compares two warehouse REITs: EastGroup Properties and Stag Industrial. EastGroup has stronger dividend growth (158% over a decade), lower debt-to-market-cap ratio (14.7%), and higher FFO growth, but trades at a premium valuation (P/FFO of 20.6). Stag Industrial offers a higher current dividend yield (4.1%), larger portfolio (120M sq ft), and lower valuation (P/FFO of 15.4), making it more attractive at current prices despite slower dividend growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong fundamentals with 14 consecutive years of dividend increases, conservative FFO payout ratio (69.2%), low debt-to-market-cap ratio (14.7%), and solid FFO growth (7.7% in 2025). However, premium valuation (P/FFO 20.6) limits upside potential.

Attractive valuation (P/FFO 15.4), higher current dividend yield (4.1%), larger diversified portfolio (120M sq ft across 41 states), and very conservative FFO payout ratio (50.9%). Monthly dividend distributions are appealing, though dividend growth has been slower than EastGroup's.

title: STAG INDUSTRIAL ANNOUNCES TAX TREATMENT OF 2025 DIVIDENDS
author: Prnewswire
description: STAG Industrial announced the tax treatment of its 2025 dividends for common stock holders. The company disclosed that ordinary taxable dividends qualify as REIT dividends under Section 199A, and capital gain distributions are subject to Section 897. The December 2025 dividend of $0.1241670 per share will be partially attributable to both 2025 and 2026 for tax purposes.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The announcement is a routine tax disclosure for dividend distributions. It provides factual information about tax treatment without indicating positive or negative business developments. The company maintains its dividend payments and portfolio of 601 buildings, which is standard operational reporting.

title: 3 ’Perfect-for-2026’ Dividends Paying Up to 11.7% Are Hiding in Plain Sight
author: Brett Owens
description: The article identifies three dividend-paying investments positioned to benefit from expected economic growth in 2026: STAG Industrial, a real estate investment trust benefiting from manufacturing automation trends; RYLD, a covered-call ETF yielding 11.7% with exposure to small-cap US growth; and Canadian Natural Resources, a heavy crude oil producer positioned to benefit from Canadian infrastructure investment and lower valuations compared to US oil majors.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Positioned as a 'pick-and-shovel' play on manufacturing automation growth with 96.8% portfolio occupancy, conservative dividend policy (59% payout ratio), and 55% total return over five years. Trading at reasonable 15x core FFO valuation with expected upside.

Offers 11.7% monthly dividend yield with exposure to US small-cap growth. Covered-call strategy benefits from volatility, and dividend has stabilized and begun rising after April tariff concerns, with further gains expected.

Trading at attractive 13.8x earnings (cheaper than US oil majors), yields 5.4% with reasonable 59% payout ratio. Record Q3 production of 1.6 million barrels per day and low operating costs of $21/barrel position it for dividend growth and stock appreciation.
Mentioned as overvalued alternative to Canadian oil plays, trading at 17.6x earnings. Article suggests Venezuela opportunity is overstated and unlikely to materially impact markets in near term.

Presented as less attractive option compared to Canadian Natural Resources, trading at higher valuation of 22.5x earnings. Article suggests investors are misplacing focus on Venezuela rather than Canadian oil sands.

title: STAG INDUSTRIAL INCREASES DIVIDEND AND SHIFTS FROM MONTHLY TO QUARTERLY CADENCE
author: Prnewswire
description: STAG Industrial Inc. announced an increase in its annual dividend rate from $1.49 to $1.55 per share and a shift from monthly to quarterly dividend payments. The first quarter 2026 dividend of $0.3875 per share will have a record date of March 31, 2026, and a payment date of April 15, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company increased its annual dividend rate by approximately 4% (from $1.49 to $1.55 per share) and shifted to a quarterly payment structure, which typically signals management confidence in cash flow generation and is generally viewed favorably by income-focused investors.

title: 3 High Dividend Stocks for Monthly Income
author: Sure Dividend
description: The article highlights three monthly dividend-paying stocks with high yields: STAG Industrial (industrial REIT with 563 buildings), Realty Income (retail REIT with 28 consecutive years of dividend increases), and Diversified Royalty (Canadian royalty firm). All three offer attractive dividend yields and demonstrate strong financial performance with growth potential.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Core FFO per share grew 8% year-over-year, beat analyst consensus, management raised guidance, and the company has significant growth runway with less than 1% market share in its $1 trillion target market.

Strong Q3 results with revenue exceeding consensus, robust acquisition activity ($5.5 billion investment volume guidance), 28 consecutive years of dividend increases (Dividend Aristocrat status), and high portfolio occupancy with financial flexibility.
